message:
test(cli/context): wrap test bodies in with_process_env_overrides_for_test for STORAGE_ROOT

Two `mod tests` cases in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/context.rs
were opening a `CliContext` against a temp dir without explicitly
pinning `STORAGE_ROOT` to that temp dir. With nothing else running
in the same process that worked, but in shared-test-runner mode
(or when an outer test left a stale process-env STORAGE_ROOT
behind), the contexts were silently picking up the wrong storage
root and running their assertions against the wrong db.

Wrap both test bodies in
`mcp_agent_mail_core::config::with_process_env_overrides_for_test(
    &[("STORAGE_ROOT", &storage_root_str)], || { ... })` so
STORAGE_ROOT is set for the duration of the test and restored
afterward. Test logic itself is unchanged — same `CliContext::
open_with_url`, same SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM projects assertion,
same ambiguous-agent-name resolve_agent assertion in the second
test (with the two same-name-different-case `INSERT INTO agents`
fixtures preserved verbatim).

Untracked artifacts test_env_read and test_guard (4 MiB ELF
scratch binaries) intentionally not staged — same skip as the
prior mcp_agent_mail_rust pass.
